title: replace docker on windows with podman, is this not built in?
body:
### Feature request description

I'm looking to move to using podman desktop on windows as opposed to rancher desktop as a number of tools now have podman support.   In rancher desktop when you turned on docker compatibility you could just execute docker commands and tools that only worked with docker continued to work.  If you turn on docker compatibility in podman desktop you can't directly execute docker it seems it still requires the docker cli to be installed?   I'm using some wordpress tools and they seem to make calls directly to docker and fail even when "docker" compatibility is turned on.  I was expecting it to add a "docker" command to the current path similar to how rancher desktop does.

### Suggest potential solution

Add a built in "docker.exe"

### Have you considered any alternatives?

It seems this is available on linux/mac just not windows

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body: podman and podman desktop are two different projects.  In Linux, some distributions (like Fedora) have a package that optionally gets installed that does this very thing.  In fact, I believe the package is installed in our machine VMs.  We do not do this for Windows, but it would seem you could set up the same symlink/alias as needed.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
